job.title: Assistant Maintenance Manager - $3,500 Sign On Bonus
job.description: At Bozzuto, every team member shares a deep commitment to doing good for those around us . We live this each day by designing, building, managing and maintaining one-of-a-kind residences. Whether it’s the talent within our communities or the expertise across our property operations teams, we work together to make extraordinary happen for our clients, residents and fellow team members. Our people are dedicated to delivering exceptional experiences to everyone we serve including each other. This s

job.description: At Bozzuto, every team member shares a deep commitment to doing good for those around us . We live this each day by designing, building, managing and maintaining one-of-a-kind residences. Whether it’s the talent within our communities or the expertise across our property operations teams, we work together to make extraordinary happen for our clients, residents and fellow team members. Our people are dedicated to delivering exceptional experiences to everyone we serve including each other. This s

We live this each day by designing, building, managing and maintaining one-of-a-kind residences. Whether it’s the talent within our communities or the expertise across our property operations teams, we work together to make extraordinary happen for our clients, residents and fellow team members. job.title: Maintenance Technician
job.description: At Bozzuto, every team member shares a deep commitment to doing good for those around us . We live this each day by designing, building, managing and maintaining one-of-a-kind residences. Whether it’s the talent within our communities or the expertise across our property operations teams, we work together to make extraordinary happen for our clients, residents and fellow team members. Our people are dedicated to delivering exceptional experiences to everyone we serve including each other. This s

job.title: Assistant General Manager
job.description:
Bozzuto's Assistant General Manager role in Annapolis sits squarely between the leasing floor and the GM's office. You're not purely a closer, and you're not purely an ops person. You do both, often on the same afternoon. That duality is the whole point of the job.

On the leasing side, you're conducting tours that actually convert. Not scripted walkthroughs, but conversations that help a prospective resident picture their life in the community. Traffic-to-lease ratios matter here. So does retention. You're working to make sure every resident interaction lands well enough that renewals feel like an easy yes rather than a negotiation.

On the financial side, you're in the rent collection process daily. Posting receipts, watching delinquency, keeping accounts receivable tight. You don't need to be an accountant, but you do need to understand why a delinquency spike at month-end is everyone's problem and how to address it before it becomes the GM's headache.

When the GM is out, you're running the show. That means coaching the team, keeping the property's interior and exterior presentation sharp, and solving resident issues without escalating everything upward. The ability to make a confident call without backup is something Bozzuto is specifically looking for here.

Weekend availability is part of the deal. One to two weekends per month, with weekday days off in exchange. That's when prospective residents are actually out looking, so it's when the leasing work gets done. It's not unusual in this business, but it's worth knowing upfront.

What separates strong AGM candidates from average ones at a company like Bozzuto isn't just sales ability. It's the combination of interpersonal range and operational discipline. Plenty of people can give a good tour. Fewer can give a great tour and then go back to the office and clean up a delinquency report without losing momentum. That combination is what this role actually demands.

What you bring

  • Four-year degree or equivalent experience in property management, hospitality, or retail
  • At least two years in a client-facing role with some exposure to rent collection, budgets, or accounts receivable
  • Comfort with Yardi or comparable property management software
  • Active presence on social platforms and the ability to create content that reflects a community's character
  • Strong written and verbal communication, and the professional polish to represent the brand on-site

The salary range runs from roughly $72,600 to $75,000, with bonus eligibility on top of that. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement. For someone looking to grow toward a General Manager seat, this role is a direct path. Bozzuto promotes from within, and the AGM position is exactly where that track starts.

job.title: Assistant General Manager
job.description:
Bozzuto runs a tight operation at the property level, and the Assistant General Manager role is where that shows most clearly. You're second in command, which means you're doing the work and leading the team, often at the same time. This position is based at a residential community in Exton, PA.

What This Role Actually Involves

Your days split across leasing, operations, and people management. You'll conduct tours with prospective residents, convert traffic into signed leases, and follow up on delinquency. Rent collection, posting receipts, and managing accounts receivable are part of your regular rhythm, not occasional tasks. You're also responsible for how the property looks, inside and out, and how the team presents itself.

When the General Manager is out, you run the site. That's not a formality. You make decisions, coach team members, and handle resident issues with real authority. Bozzuto expects you to step in without needing a script.

Weekend availability is part of this role. One to two weekends per month, with weekday days off in exchange. Prospective residents shop apartments on weekends, and someone senior needs to be there.

What You Need to Bring

  • A four-year degree or equivalent experience in property management, hospitality, or retail
  • At least two years in a customer-facing, operations-oriented role
  • Hands-on experience with rent collection, budgets, and accounts receivable
  • Comfort with Yardi or similar property management software
  • Active presence on social media platforms, with the ability to create content that actually connects with people
  • Strong written and verbal communication. You're representing the community in every interaction.
  • A sales instinct. You should be able to read a prospect, build rapport quickly, and close.

Where This Role Can Take You

The AGM seat at a company like Bozzuto is a proving ground. Strong performers here typically move into General Manager roles, which means full P&L ownership, direct oversight of NOI, and accountability for the entire site's performance. The skills you build here, delinquency management, leasing conversion, team leadership under pressure, transfer directly to that next level. If you want to run your own property, this is the track.

What separates a strong AGM candidate from an average one isn't just leasing ability. It's the combination of financial literacy and people instincts. You need to care about occupancy numbers and genuinely enjoy talking to residents. Both matter here, and neither alone is enough.

Compensation for this role falls between $68,000 and $70,000 annually, with bonus eligibility on top of base pay. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement.

job.title: Assistant Maintenance Manager
job.description:
Maintenance operations at multifamily properties only work when someone bridges the gap between hands-on trade work and team coordination. That's what this role does. Bozzuto is hiring an Assistant Maintenance Manager at their Atlanta property to keep the building running, the turns moving, and the team accountable.

What the Job Actually Looks Like

You're splitting your time between wrench work and oversight. On any given day, that means diagnosing an HVAC issue in one unit, checking punch list progress on a make-ready down the hall, then following up on a resident service ticket that's been open too long. You're not just fixing things. You're making sure the whole maintenance operation holds its standard.

The role covers the full trades spectrum: HVAC, plumbing, electrical, appliance repair, basic carpentry, drywall, and painting. Exposure to complex mechanical systems like central plant, chillers, or boilers is a plus. Preventative maintenance programs are yours to execute consistently, not just check off a list.

The make-ready process is a big piece of this. Turns need to move fast and finish clean so leasing can do its job. You'll manage that flow with a sharp eye for detail and a real sense of urgency.

On-call rotation is part of the deal. Weekends too. That's the job, and strong candidates go in knowing it.

What Bozzuto Needs From You

  • 5 or more years in the trades, facilities, or residential building maintenance
  • HVAC certification, EPA certification, or NAPE certification (at least one)
  • Solid working knowledge of OSHA, MSDS, and risk management basics
  • Some supervisory experience, or a clear track record of leading peers informally
  • Comfort with property management technology platforms and basic Microsoft Office tools
  • Physical capability to handle the demands of the role: lifting up to 50 pounds frequently, climbing ladders, working outdoors in varying conditions

Pay runs $27 to $29 per hour, with bonus eligibility. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement.

The candidates who stand out in this role aren't just technically sharp. They're the ones who treat a resident's work order with the same seriousness they bring to a boiler inspection. That combination of trade skill and people accountability is what separates a good maintenance tech from someone ready to step into a leadership track. From here, the natural next move is a Service Manager or Maintenance Director role, and Bozzuto's size means those opportunities exist within the same portfolio.

job.title: Assistant Maintenance Manager
job.description:
Bozzuto has built a reputation across the Mid-Atlantic and Pacific Northwest for communities that feel genuinely well-run. That reputation depends heavily on maintenance teams who treat asset preservation as a craft, not a checklist. This Assistant Maintenance Manager role in Issaquah is for someone who brings real trade depth to the work and can translate that depth into a team culture where standards don't slip.

The job sits at the intersection of hands-on technical work and front-line supervision. You're not purely a wrench-turner, and you're not purely a desk manager. On any given day you might be troubleshooting an HVAC issue in the morning, walking a punch list on a make-ready unit at midday, and coaching a maintenance tech on work order documentation before you leave. The make-ready process matters here: vacant units cost money, and the ability to manage turns with precision directly affects occupancy and the leasing team's ability to convert traffic into signed leases.

Preventative maintenance isn't glamorous, but it's where the real asset protection happens. Bozzuto expects you to own that program, not just participate in it. Exposure to complex mechanical systems, whether central plant, boilers, or split systems, is genuinely useful in this building. If you've only ever worked in simple garden-style product, it's worth being honest with yourself about the learning curve here.

The on-call rotation is real. Emergencies don't schedule themselves, and this role participates in that coverage. If that's new information, it shouldn't be. It's standard for any maintenance management role, and the candidates who handle it well are the ones who've already made peace with occasional off-hours calls rather than being surprised by them.

What separates strong candidates from average ones here is the leadership piece. Plenty of people have the technical credentials. Fewer have the instinct to correct a team member's work in a way that actually improves their skills rather than just fixing the immediate problem. Bozzuto's culture puts a premium on that kind of mentorship, and it shows in how they describe this role internally.

What you'll need to bring

  • 5 or more years in the trades, facilities, or residential building maintenance
  • HVAC, EPA, and/or NAPE certification
  • Working knowledge of HVAC, plumbing, electrical systems, and appliance repair
  • Familiarity with OSHA standards and risk management practices
  • Basic carpentry, drywall, and painting skills
  • Prior supervisory experience, or a demonstrated readiness to lead
  • Comfort with technology platforms and work order management systems
  • Availability for emergency on-call rotation and weekend work
  • Physical ability to lift 25 to 50 pounds frequently and work in varying weather conditions

The salary range runs from $60,000 to just under $67,000, with bonus eligibility, medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement. For someone who wants to grow toward a Maintenance Manager or Director of Facilities role, this is a reasonable place to build that track record with an operator that invests in development.

job.title: Assistant Maintenance Manager
job.description:
Skilled trades knowledge only gets you so far in this role. What actually makes the difference here is the ability to hold a maintenance operation together through your technical depth, your eye for detail, and your capacity to get other people working at the same standard you hold yourself to. This is a hands-on supervisory position, and the balance between doing and leading is constant.

What This Role Demands Day to Day

The work splits into three broad categories: reactive, preventative, and administrative. On the reactive side, you're resolving HVAC, electrical, plumbing, and appliance issues with the kind of competence that comes from real field experience, not just book knowledge. Preventative work means running scheduled inspections and maintenance programs that protect the building's long-term value. The administrative layer is lighter but real: service ticket tracking, code compliance, safety protocols, and enough comfort with technology platforms to stay organized.

The turn process is a significant part of the job. Getting make-ready units through punch list and back to leasable condition on time directly affects occupancy, and Bozzuto operates at a level where that timeline matters. You're not just completing turns yourself. You're making sure the team completes them correctly.

Oakland's housing stock includes a real mix of building vintages, and this role specifically calls out experience with complex mechanical systems: central plant, boilers, chillers, split systems. That's not incidental. Candidates who've only worked garden-style wood-frame product may find the mechanical expectations here a step up.

This position also includes weekend availability and emergency on-call rotation. That's standard for roles at this level, but worth naming plainly. The building doesn't stop needing attention because it's Saturday night.

Skills and Background That Fit

  • Five or more years in the trades, facilities, or residential building maintenance
  • HVAC, EPA, and/or NAPE certification
  • Working knowledge of OSHA, MSDS, and risk management practices
  • Hands-on proficiency across HVAC, plumbing, electrical, and appliance repair
  • Basic carpentry, drywall, and painting skills
  • Supervisory experience or strong leadership aptitude
  • Comfort with Microsoft Office and property management technology platforms
  • Physical ability to handle frequent lifting in the 25 to 50 pound range, stair climbing, ladder use, and outdoor work in variable weather

What's Distinctive About This Role at Bozzuto

Bozzuto has a reputation in the managed properties space for running tight operations with genuine attention to resident experience. The "preserve and protect" framing in this role isn't just marketing language. It reflects how the maintenance function is positioned internally: not as a cost center to minimize, but as a direct contributor to resident satisfaction and asset value. For a maintenance professional who takes pride in the physical condition of a property and wants that mindset recognized, that cultural alignment is worth something.

The salary range for this position is $80,000 to $87,000, with bonus eligibility, 20 days of paid time off, medical, dental, and vision coverage, 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement.

job.title: Assistant Maintenance Manager
job.description:
Bozzuto operates some of the more well-regarded residential communities in the country, and the Boston property tied to this role reflects that standard. The Assistant Maintenance Manager position sits between hands-on trade work and team leadership. You're not just fixing things. You're setting the pace for everyone around you.

Day to day, you're working through service tickets, keeping HVAC systems, plumbing, and electrical in good shape, and making sure the make-ready process runs tight. Turns need to close fast. Punch lists need to actually get punched. When a unit sits because a make-ready dragged on, that's a leasing problem, and you'll understand that instinctively.

Preventative maintenance is a real part of this job, not an afterthought. You'll own the PM schedule alongside the maintenance manager, track building systems, and stay ahead of issues before residents feel them. Boston winters add complexity here. Snow removal, mechanical system performance under cold stress, and keeping the property clean and safe when conditions get rough are all on your plate.

The on-call rotation is real. So are weekend shifts. If that's a dealbreaker, this role isn't the right fit. But if you're used to that rhythm and you take pride in being the person who shows up when something goes wrong, this is a position where that matters.

What separates strong candidates here isn't just trade knowledge. It's the combination of technical skill and the ability to hold a team accountable without micromanaging. Bozzuto runs a culture-forward operation, and they'll notice quickly whether you lead by example or just talk about it.

What you'll need

  • 5 or more years in the trades, facilities, or residential building maintenance
  • HVAC, EPA, and/or NAPE certification
  • Solid working knowledge of plumbing, electrical, and appliance repair
  • Familiarity with complex mechanical systems: boilers, chillers, central plant, or split systems is a plus
  • Basic carpentry, drywall, and painting skills
  • OSHA awareness and a genuine safety-first mindset
  • Supervisory experience, or a clear track record of leading peers informally
  • Comfort with technology platforms and basic Microsoft Office proficiency
  • Physical ability to lift 25 to 50 pounds regularly and work in varying weather conditions

The salary range is $65,000 to $70,000, with bonus eligibility on top of that. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement.

This role is a natural stepping stone toward a full Maintenance Manager position. Candidates who use this role to build out their preventative maintenance discipline and sharpen their leadership skills tend to move up within Bozzuto's portfolio relatively quickly.

job.title: Assistant Maintenance Manager
job.description:
What You'll Actually Do

This role sits between the hands-on work and the management layer, which means your days won't look the same twice. One morning you're troubleshooting an HVAC unit that stopped cooling overnight. By afternoon you're walking a make-ready with your team, checking punch list items before a new resident moves in. The job demands both technical depth and the ability to get other people moving in the right direction.

Bozzuto operates with a "preserve and protect" mentality, and that philosophy shapes the daily rhythm here. You'll handle service tickets across HVAC, plumbing, electrical, and appliance repair, and you'll be expected to close them out with real follow-through, not just a checkbox. The make-ready process is a core part of this position. Turns need to be tight. New residents notice everything, and a sloppy turn creates a trust deficit that the leasing and management teams spend weeks recovering from.

Preventative maintenance is another major piece. Executing PM programs consistently is what keeps a property from sliding into reactive-only mode, which is expensive, exhausting, and bad for asset value. You'll also handle grounds upkeep, snow removal, and general safety and cleanliness standards. And yes, you're on the emergency on-call rotation. Nights and weekends happen in this work. That's not a footnote.

What You Bring

  • At least 5 years in the trades, facilities, or residential building maintenance
  • Solid hands-on skills across HVAC, plumbing, electrical, and appliance repair
  • HVAC certification, EPA certification, and/or NAPE certification
  • Familiarity with OSHA standards, risk management principles, and MSDS
  • Basic carpentry, drywall, and painting skills
  • Exposure to complex mechanical systems (central plant, chillers, boilers, split systems) is a plus
  • Supervisory or leadership experience, even informal, matters here
  • Comfort with technology platforms and basic Microsoft Office proficiency
  • Physical ability to lift 25 to 50 pounds frequently, climb ladders, and work in varying weather

The Bigger Picture

What separates strong candidates from average ones in this role isn't purely technical skill. Plenty of maintenance techs can fix a leak. The people who thrive at the assistant manager level are the ones who can communicate clearly with residents after something went wrong, coach a junior tech without micromanaging, and spot a small problem before it becomes a capex conversation. If you're the person on your current team who others quietly come to when something complicated breaks, that's a good sign this role fits you.

The salary range for this position is $65,000 to $70,000, with additional bonus opportunities. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement.

job.title: Assistant Maintenance Manager
job.description:
The Role in Plain Terms

This is a hands-on maintenance leadership position at a Bozzuto residential community in East Boston. You're not just fixing things. You're running the floor, guiding your team, and making sure the building stays in shape from the mechanical room to the grounds. The make-ready process, preventative maintenance, and emergency response all run through you.

What You'll Actually Be Doing

Your days split between technical work and team oversight. You're resolving HVAC, plumbing, electrical, and appliance issues yourself when needed, but you're also checking that your team closes service requests completely and on time. Residents notice the follow-through. Sloppy ticket closure shows up in reviews and renewal rates.

Turn management is a core part of this job. When a unit goes vacant, you're coordinating the make-ready from punch list to final walk so leasing can put it back on the market without delays. A slow turn costs the property real money.

  • Execute and oversee preventative maintenance schedules for all building systems
  • Maintain compliance with applicable building codes, OSHA standards, and safety protocols
  • Handle general upkeep including grounds maintenance and snow removal
  • Participate in the emergency on-call rotation and weekend coverage
  • Work with complex mechanical systems including boilers, chillers, and central plant equipment

East Boston properties deal with older building stock and New England winters. If you've managed boiler systems and handled cold-weather grounds operations, that experience translates directly here.

What Bozzuto Expects You to Bring

  • 5 or more years in the trades, facilities, or residential building maintenance
  • HVAC, EPA, and/or NAPE certification
  • Working knowledge of OSHA, MSDS, and risk management practices
  • Hands-on proficiency across HVAC, plumbing, electrical, and appliance repair
  • Basic carpentry, drywall, and paint skills
  • Supervisory experience or a demonstrated ability to lead a small crew
  • Comfort with technology platforms and Microsoft Office basics
  • Physical capability to lift 25 to 50 pounds frequently and work in varying weather

The candidates who stand out aren't just technically sharp. They communicate clearly with residents, hold their team accountable without micromanaging, and treat the property like they own it. That "know it like you built it" mentality matters at Bozzuto because it shows in the condition of the asset over time.

Compensation and Growth

The salary range is $67,000 to $69,000, with bonus eligibility on top of that. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement.

For someone with strong technical skills who's ready to move into full maintenance management, this role is a direct path. Bozzuto promotes internally and the company manages properties at scale across multiple markets. An Assistant Maintenance Manager who builds a clean record here, keeps NOI protected through solid preventative maintenance, and develops a reliable team has a clear route to a Maintenance Manager title.

job.title: Assistant Maintenance Manager
job.description:
What You'll Actually Do

This role lives at the intersection of hands-on technical work and frontline leadership. You're not just fixing things. You're setting the standard for how the entire maintenance operation runs on a day-to-day basis at a Bozzuto community in Washington, DC.

A significant part of your week will involve resolving service tickets across HVAC, plumbing, electrical, and appliance repair. Timeliness and follow-through matter here because residents notice both. You'll also own a share of the make-ready process, keeping turns moving on schedule so vacant units are leased-ready and new residents move into a polished home. That punch list mentality has to be second nature.

Beyond reactive work, you'll execute preventative maintenance programs that protect the long-term value of the asset. Bozzuto's communities are high-quality properties, and that quality doesn't maintain itself. You'll handle grounds upkeep, snow removal, general cleanliness, and compliance with applicable building and safety codes. Emergency on-call rotation and weekend availability are part of the deal. That's the honest reality of residential maintenance at this level, and it's worth knowing upfront.

You'll also be coaching and motivating the people around you. The supervisory piece isn't secondary to the technical piece. Both carry equal weight.

What You Bring

  • At least 5 years of experience in the trades, facilities maintenance, or residential building operations
  • Demonstrated proficiency in HVAC, plumbing, electrical systems, and appliance repair
  • HVAC, EPA, and/or NAPE certification
  • Familiarity with OSHA standards, risk management principles, and MSDS protocols
  • Basic carpentry, drywall, and paint skills
  • Exposure to complex mechanical systems like boilers, chillers, or central plant equipment is a meaningful plus
  • Some supervisory or leadership experience, even informal, that shows you can direct and develop others
  • Comfort with technology platforms and basic Microsoft Office proficiency
  • Physical ability to frequently lift 25 to 50 pounds, climb stairs and ladders, and work in variable weather conditions

Where This Role Takes You

Strong Assistant Maintenance Managers at companies like Bozzuto typically progress into Building Maintenance Manager roles, where you hold full P&L accountability for the maintenance budget and lead a larger team independently. The skills you'll sharpen here, particularly preventative maintenance planning, vendor coordination, and team accountability, transfer directly into that next step. If you have the technical background but haven't yet had a formal leadership title, this role is a real opportunity to build that track record with a company that has the portfolio size to promote from within.

The salary range for this position is $67,000 to $72,000, with additional bonus opportunities and a benefits package that includes medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement.

job.title: Assistant Maintenance Manager - $2500 Sign on Bonus
job.description:
Bozzuto has built a reputation for running well-maintained, well-managed communities, and the Assistant Maintenance Manager role is where that reputation gets earned every day. This is a hands-on leadership position at a property in Chapel Hill, NC, with a $2,500 sign-on bonus for the right candidate.

What the Work Actually Looks Like

Your day pulls in a lot of directions. You're turning wrenches on HVAC systems in the morning, walking a punch list on a make-ready unit after lunch, and coaching a technician on proper documentation before the end of your shift. The make-ready process is a big part of this role. Turns need to move quickly and cleanly so leasing has something to show and new residents can move in on schedule. You'll own that process end to end, and the quality of your punch list work directly affects resident satisfaction from day one.

Beyond turns, you'll manage preventative maintenance programs to protect the asset long-term, handle service tickets across HVAC, plumbing, electrical, and appliance repair, and keep common areas and grounds in shape. Snow removal and exterior upkeep are part of the job too. This property operates with real mechanical complexity, so familiarity with central plant systems, boilers, chillers, or split systems is a genuine advantage here, not just a checkbox.

Weekend availability and participation in the emergency on-call rotation are real expectations. That's standard for maintenance leadership at this level, and worth knowing upfront.

What Bozzuto Is Looking For

  • 5 or more years in the trades, facilities, or residential building maintenance
  • HVAC, EPA, or NAPE certification (required)
  • Working knowledge of plumbing, electrical, and appliance repair
  • Solid understanding of OSHA, MSDS, and risk management principles
  • Basic carpentry, drywall, and painting skills
  • Leadership experience or a strong aptitude for supervising others
  • Comfortable with technology platforms, work order systems, and Microsoft Office
  • Ability to lift 25 to 50 pounds frequently and work in varying weather conditions

Why This Role Is Worth Your Attention

Chapel Hill sits in a competitive rental market with a heavy university influence and consistent demand. Bozzuto operates Class A and B communities, and their maintenance standards reflect that. You're not patching things together here. You're expected to know the building like you built it, which is both a higher bar and a more satisfying way to work.

Strong candidates in this role are the ones who can shift between doing the technical work themselves and developing the people around them. The assistant manager who grows fastest is usually the one who treats every service call as a training moment for junior techs, not just a task to close out. This position has a natural path toward a full Maintenance Manager role as you demonstrate that supervisory range.

Compensation runs $28 to $30 per hour, plus the sign-on bonus. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off, 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement.
